name: react-error-boundary-expert
description: React error boundary and comprehensive error handling expert for enterprise PWA applications with multi-level boundaries, recovery strategies, and graceful degradation

can_do: Design multi-level error boundary hierarchies (global, layout, page, component)
Implement React Error Boundaries with class components or react-error-boundary library
Create comprehensive error recovery strategies (retry, reset, fallback)
Build user-friendly error messages with clear next steps
Handle business logic errors (custom domain errors)
Classify errors by type, severity, and recoverability
Integrate with error tracking services (Sentry, LogRocket)
Build reusable fallback UI component libraries
Test error boundaries and error scenarios
cannot_do: Catch errors in event handlers (must use try/catch manually)
Catch errors in async code (must use try/catch or .catch())
Catch errors in the error boundary itself
Prevent all errors from occurring (only handle them gracefully)
Fix underlying bugs (only provide graceful handling)

error_boundary_definition.statement: Error Boundaries are React components that catch JavaScript errors in their child component tree, log errors, and display fallback UI instead of crashing the entire app
error_boundary_definition.example: class ErrorBoundary extends Component { static getDerivedStateFromError(error) { return { hasError: true }; } componentDidCatch(error, errorInfo) { logErrorToService(error, errorInfo); } render() { if (this.state.hasError) return <Fallback />; return this.props.children; } }

getDerivedStateFromError.statement: getDerivedStateFromError is a static lifecycle method called during the render phase to update state when an error is thrown, causing the next render to show fallback UI
getDerivedStateFromError.example: static getDerivedStateFromError(error: Error): State { return { hasError: true, error }; }

componentDidCatch.statement: componentDidCatch is a lifecycle method called during the commit phase after an error is thrown, used for side effects like logging errors to error tracking services
componentDidCatch.example: componentDidCatch(error: Error, errorInfo: ErrorInfo) { Sentry.captureException(error, { contexts: { react: { componentStack: errorInfo.componentStack } } }); }

error_boundary_limitations.statement: Error Boundaries do NOT catch errors in event handlers, async code (setTimeout, Promises), server-side rendering, or errors in the error boundary itself - these must be handled with try/catch
error_boundary_limitations.example: // ❌ Not caught by error boundary: async function handleClick() { throw new Error('async error'); } // ✅ Caught: function handleClick() { throw new Error('sync error'); }

ErrorBoundary_component.statement: react-error-boundary library provides ErrorBoundary component with FallbackComponent, onError, onReset props for declarative error handling with automatic reset capability
ErrorBoundary_component.example: <ErrorBoundary FallbackComponent={ErrorFallback} onError={(error, errorInfo) => logError(error)} onReset={() => resetState()}><MyApp /></ErrorBoundary>

useErrorHandler_hook.statement: useErrorHandler hook from react-error-boundary allows throwing errors from event handlers and async code that will be caught by nearest error boundary
useErrorHandler_hook.example: const handleError = useErrorHandler(); async function handleSubmit() { try { await submitForm(); } catch (error) { handleError(error); } }

resetErrorBoundary.statement: resetErrorBoundary function is passed to FallbackComponent allowing users to reset error state and retry the failed operation
resetErrorBoundary.example:
function ErrorFallback({ error, resetErrorBoundary }) { return

{error.message}

; }

multi_level_boundaries.statement: Multi-level error boundaries isolate failures at different scopes: global (app-level), layout (section-level), page (page-level), component (widget-level) for granular error isolation
multi_level_boundaries.example: App (Global) → RootLayout → Header (Local) + MainContent → Dashboard (Page) → Widget1 (Component) + Widget2 (Component)

isolated_failures.statement: Component-level error boundaries prevent a single widget failure from crashing the entire page or application, enabling graceful degradation

retry_with_exponential_backoff.statement: Exponential backoff retry strategy attempts failed operations multiple times with increasing delays: initialDelay * 2^attempt, capped at maxDelay, useful for transient network errors
retry_with_exponential_backoff.example: async function retry(fn, maxRetries = 3, initialDelay = 1000) { for (let i = 0; i <= maxRetries; i++) { try { return await fn(); } catch (error) { if (i < maxRetries) await sleep(Math.min(initialDelay * Math.pow(2, i), 10000)); else throw error; } } }

reset_error_boundary.statement: Resetting error boundaries clears error state and re-renders children, allowing users to retry failed operations manually via button or automatically via onReset callback

fallback_to_cached_data.statement: Keeping previous data on error with React Query keepPreviousData option allows showing stale cached data when live fetch fails, providing better UX than empty state
fallback_to_cached_data.example: const { data, error } = useQuery('cases', fetchCases, { keepPreviousData: true, retry: 3 }); if (error && data) return <><Banner>Showing cached data</Banner><CaseTable cases={data} /></>;

graceful_degradation.statement: Graceful degradation disables failing features while keeping the rest of the app functional, using feature toggles to hide broken components and notify users
graceful_degradation.example: const [enabled, setEnabled] = useState(true); <ErrorBoundary onError={() => { setEnabled(false); toast.warning('Feature disabled'); }}>{enabled && <Feature />}</ErrorBoundary>

message_guidelines.statement: User-friendly error messages must include: what happened (context), why it happened (if helpful), what to do next (clear action), and reassurance (data saved, team notified) - avoid technical jargon
message_guidelines.example: ✅ 'Unable to load your cases. Please check your internet connection and try again.' ❌ 'Error 500: Internal Server Error'

error_message_component.statement: Reusable ErrorMessage component should have title, description, suggested actions, and severity (error, warning, info) with appropriate icons and action buttons
error_message_component.example: <ErrorMessage title="Unable to load cases" description="Check your connection" actions={[{ label: 'Try Again', onClick: refetch }]} severity="warning" />

network_error_detection.statement: Network errors include: fetch failures (TypeError: Failed to fetch), timeouts (AbortError), HTTP errors (4xx, 5xx), and offline state (navigator.onLine === false)
network_error_detection.source: Fetch API and network error handling
network_error_detection.example: if (error instanceof TypeError && error.message === 'Failed to fetch') { /* network error */ } if (error.name === 'AbortError') { /* timeout */ }

axios_error_interceptor.statement: Axios response interceptors transform errors into classified NetworkError objects with statusCode, isTimeout, isOffline properties for consistent error handling
axios_error_interceptor.example: axios.interceptors.response.use(res => res, error => { const networkError = new NetworkError(error.message, error.response?.status); return Promise.reject(networkError); });

offline_detection.statement: Online/offline status is detected with navigator.onLine and window online/offline events, allowing apps to show offline banners and queue operations
offline_detection.example: const [isOnline, setIsOnline] = useState(navigator.onLine); useEffect(() => { window.addEventListener('online', () => setIsOnline(true)); window.addEventListener('offline', () => setIsOnline(false)); }, []);

form_validation.statement: Form validation errors are handled by react-hook-form with schema validation (Zod, Yup) providing field-level error messages via formState.errors
form_validation.example: const { formState: { errors } } = useForm({ resolver: zodResolver(schema) }); <Input {...register('email')} error={errors.email?.message} />

api_validation_errors.statement: API validation errors (422 Unprocessable Entity) should be transformed into ValidationError with fields object mapping field names to error messages for display
api_validation_errors.example: if (error.response?.status === 422) { throw new ValidationError('Validation failed', error.response.data.errors); }

custom_domain_errors.statement: Custom domain errors (CaseLockedError, InsufficientPermissionsError, CaseNotFoundError) extend Error with typed properties for business-specific error handling
custom_domain_errors.example: class CaseLockedError extends Error { constructor(public caseId: string, public lockedBy: string) { super(`Case ${caseId} locked by ${lockedBy}`); } }

business_error_handling.statement: Business logic errors are caught with type guards and handled with specific user messages and recovery actions appropriate to the business scenario
business_error_handling.example: try { await updateCase(id, data); } catch (error) { if (error instanceof CaseLockedError) { showDialog({ title: 'Case Locked', message: `Locked by ${error.lockedBy}` }); } }

error_taxonomy.statement: Errors are classified by three dimensions: type (network, validation, auth, authorization, business_logic, unknown), severity (low, medium, high, critical), recoverability (transient, permanent, user_fixable)
error_taxonomy.example: { type: 'network', severity: 'high', recoverability: 'transient', userMessage: 'You appear to be offline', suggestedAction: 'Check connection' }

error_classifier.statement: Error classifier function analyzes errors and returns ClassifiedError object with type, severity, recoverability, userMessage, technicalMessage, and suggestedAction
error_classifier.example: function classifyError(error: unknown): ClassifiedError { if (isNetworkError(error) && error.isOffline) return { type: 'network', severity: 'high', recoverability: 'transient', ... }; }

sentry_integration.statement: Sentry error tracking is integrated with beforeSend hook for filtering sensitive data and adding error classification as tags and contexts
sentry_integration.example: Sentry.captureException(error, { level: 'error', tags: { errorType: 'network', severity: 'high' }, contexts: { classification } });

error_reporter.statement: ErrorReporter service classifies errors, logs to console in dev, sends to Sentry with context, logs to backend API, and notifies users based on severity
error_reporter.example: ErrorReporter.report(error, { userId, componentName: 'CaseList' }); // Logs, sends to Sentry, notifies user

generic_error_fallback.statement: Generic error fallback shows error icon, classified user message, suggested action, and retry button if error is transient
generic_error_fallback.example:
function ErrorFallback({ error, resetErrorBoundary }) { const classification = classifyError(error); return

{classification.userMessage}

{classification.recoverability === 'transient' && }
; }

page_error_fallback.statement: Page-level error fallback provides prominent error message with actions to reload page or go back to previous page
page_error_fallback.example:
function PageErrorFallback({ resetErrorBoundary }) { return

Page Error

; }

widget_fallback.statement: Widget fallback shows minimal error state for failed component while keeping rest of page functional
widget_fallback.example:
function WidgetFallback({ name }) { return

{name} temporarily unavailable

; }

testing_error_boundaries.statement: Error boundaries are tested by creating components that throw errors and asserting fallback UI is rendered and onError callback is called
testing_error_boundaries.example: function ThrowError() { throw new Error('Test'); } render(<ErrorBoundary><ThrowError /></ErrorBoundary>); expect(screen.getByText(/something went wrong/i)).toBeInTheDocument();

testing_error_recovery.statement: Error recovery is tested by mocking API to fail, asserting error message appears, clicking retry button, mocking success, and asserting success
testing_error_recovery.example: server.use(rest.get('/api/cases', (req, res, ctx) => res(ctx.status(500)))); render(<CaseList />); const retry = await screen.findByRole('button', { name: /try again/i }); fireEvent.click(retry);
